title: Amihud illiquidity lambda (volume-based feature, AFML Ch19.4.2)
source: LdP literature mining (2 parallel read-only agents over AFML Ch5/17/18/19 + HFT, books in D:/PROJECTS/QuantTradingDocs/extracted), 2026-06-03. Conversation: 'hay alguna otra nueva feature que se puede extraer de la literatura'.
why: The project uses NO volume-based feature today. Amihud lambda = rolling mean of |return|/(price*volume) (AFML §19.4.2) is the first volume/liquidity signal; the book validates it specifically for DAILY data (Hasbrouck 2009: daily Amihud rank-correlates to intraday effective spread). CAVEAT: MT5 'volume' on XAU is TICK COUNT not dollar volume, so this is a tick-volume proxy — informative for relative illiquidity over time but must be labeled as a proxy.
value_rationale: WORTH-TESTING. Cheap, causal, brings the first volume dimension; prefer the rolling t-value over the mean (AFML §19.4 advice).
scope: Implement rolling Amihud = mean(|log_ret| / (close*volume)) over a causal window; expose its rolling t-value
Label explicitly as tick-volume proxy on XAU (not literal dollar-volume)
Rolling window only, no full-sample mean (causality)
recommendation: Test on D1 as the first volume feature; honest proxy labeling.
out_of_scope: Kyle lambda on D1 (degenerates to noisy Amihud without aggressor flags — defer to H4 w/ BVC).

history.reason: LdP literature mining: Amihud illiquidity = first volume-based feature (tick-volume proxy on XAU)
verification: prefer Kyle lambda t-value (B0141) as headline microstructure feature; reformulate Amihud to DOLLAR-volume + t-value; downgrade priority below B0141
